note: TableField Cellspan is a config-free submodule of tablefield that merges rendered table cells via #colspan#/#rowspan#/#remove# markers in a hook_preprocess_table() implementation. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: Using the TableField Cellspan module, how do I make one table cell span two columns to its side? What exactly do I type, and where?
evals.expected_output: Type the literal marker #colspan# as the text of the right-hand cell (in the TableField grid); the module deletes that cell and grows the colspan of the cell immediately to its left.

evals.prompt: How does TableField Cellspan let a cell span multiple rows vertically, and which marker do I use?
evals.expected_output: Put #rowspan# in the cell below; it is removed and the nearest non-empty cell above in the same column gets its rowspan increased. Repeat #rowspan# in each row underneath to span further.

evals.prompt: With TableField Cellspan, which marker deletes a leftover table cell entirely so no <td> is output?
evals.expected_output: The #remove# marker: a cell containing it is unset from the rows so no cell is rendered, used to clear leftover cells a span leaves behind.

evals.prompt: How does TableField Cellspan turn markers into real colspan/rowspan attributes at render time? Which Drupal hook implements it?
evals.expected_output: It implements hook_preprocess_table() (tablefield_cellspan_preprocess_table) which rewrites $variables['rows'] cell attributes; the stored field value is not changed.

evals.prompt: On a TableField Cellspan site, why do the merged cells appear on the public page but not in the field editor? What guards this?
evals.expected_output: The preprocess only runs on front-end routes: it returns early when router.admin_context->isAdminRoute() is true, so admin/edit pages are left unmerged.

evals.prompt: What configuration or settings form does the TableField Cellspan module provide, and what permissions does it define?
evals.expected_output: None. The module has no menu, no settings/config, no schema, and no permissions; enabling it (with tablefield) is the entire setup and behavior is driven by the cell markers.
